output.summary: Change .top-gradient height from 150vw to 150vh and add JavaScript to cap gradient max-height to the parent <main> element height, with a ResizeObserver to update dynamically.

fileChanges.details: Update the .top-gradient rule: change `height: 150vw` to `height: 150vh`. This changes the gradient from viewport-width-relative to viewport-height-relative sizing.

fileChanges.description: Add inline JavaScript to cap gradient max-height to <main> element height
fileChanges.details: Add a <script> block after the .top-gradient div. The script should: (1) Find the closest <main> ancestor of the hero block, or fall back to document.querySelector('main'). (2) If a <main> element exists, set the .top-gradient element's max-height CSS property to the <main> element's offsetHeight in pixels. (3) Attach a ResizeObserver to the <main> element to update the max-height whenever <main> changes size. (4) Clean up gracefully — use an IIFE scoped to the current block instance so multiple hero blocks on a page don't conflict. The script should be minimal and run immediately (no DOMContentLoaded needed since it's inline after the element).

designDecisions.question: Should the JS use ResizeObserver or a simpler approach like window resize listener?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use ResizeObserver on the <main> element. ResizeObserver is more accurate than window resize because <main> height can change due to content changes (e.g., accordions expanding, dynamic content loading) without the window resizing. ResizeObserver has excellent browser support (97%+).

designDecisions.question: How should the script be scoped to avoid conflicts with multiple hero blocks on the same page?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use an IIFE that receives the current script's previousElementSibling (the .top-gradient div) as context. Each hero block gets its own script tag and its own observer instance.

designDecisions.question: Should the TSX editor component also reflect the 150vh change?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes, the CSS file is shared between editor and frontend. The height change from 150vw to 150vh applies automatically in both. The JS max-height capping is frontend-only (PHP) and does not need a TSX equivalent since the editor doesn't have a <main> ancestor context.
